fa: Larian's massive RPG swept most major awards in 2023, winning GOTY at The Game Awards, BAFTA, and the D.I.C.E. Awards in a historic clean sweep.
The game redefined the CRPG genre with its unprecedented reactivity to player choices, full co-op support, and hundreds of hours of authored content.
Elden Ring dominated 2022 awards, winning GOTY at TGA, BAFTA, and D.I.C.E., driven by the collaboration between Hidetaka Miyazaki and author George R.R. Martin.
The Lands Between featured breathtaking vistas and a cohesive, tragic mythology expressed entirely through environmental storytelling.
Josef Fares's cooperative platformer was considered a major upset winner, beating out Deathloop and Metroid Dread in what many called the most competitive GOTY race in years.
Returnal's fractured, unreliable narrative delivered through environmental storytelling in a roguelike structure was recognized as a breakthrough in game storytelling.

n: The Last of Us Part II
fa: The Last of Us Part II won 7 awards at The Game Awards 2020, the most wins in a single night in TGA history, despite being one of the most divisive games in recent memory.

n: The Last of Us Part II
fa: The game's controversial dual-protagonist structure, which forces players to play as a character who commits a shocking act early in the story, defined debates about narrative in games.
Hades became the first video game to win the Hugo Award for Best Game, and its 4 wins at The Game Awards 2020 made it the most award-winning indie game of the year.
Sekiro's win over the widely expected Death Stranding surprised many, recognizing FromSoftware's innovative parry-based combat system and Japanese feudal setting.
Norman Reedus won Best Performance for portraying Sam Bridges, in a game that pushed the boundaries of cinematic acting capture in video games.
The soft reboot of God of War, shifting from Greek to Norse mythology and adding Kratos's son Atreus as a central character, was universally celebrated and won over 200 Game of the Year awards.
The father-son dynamic between Kratos and Atreus was praised as one of gaming's most emotionally resonant stories, redefining what action games could accomplish narratively.

n: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
fa: BotW swept virtually every Game of the Year award in 2017, winning at TGA, D.I.C.E., GDC, IGN, GameSpot, and nearly every major publication for its revolutionary open-world design.

n: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
fa: The GDC recognized BotW's emergent physics-based puzzle design, which allowed players to solve challenges in ways designers had not anticipated.
Overwatch won GOTY at The Game Awards despite only launching in May 2016, fueled by its diverse hero roster, polished team-based gameplay, and record 35 million players in its first year.
Overwatch redefined the hero shooter genre and was praised for making competitive multiplayer accessible to players who had never engaged with the format before.
The Witcher 3 won over 800 individual Game of the Year awards globally in 2015 - the most ever awarded to a single title - setting a Guinness World Record.
The game's mature, morally complex storytelling set a new benchmark for open-world RPG writing, with both main story and side quests receiving near-universal praise.
Dragon Age: Inquisition took home GOTY at The Game Awards 2014, TGA's inaugural year, recognized for its expansive world and ambitious party-based RPG design.
The Last of Us won 249 Game of the Year awards in 2013 - a record at the time - and is credited with establishing that video games could achieve the emotional weight of prestige television.
Gustavo Santaolalla's acoustic guitar score for The Last of Us was celebrated for its restraint and emotional precision, becoming one of gaming's most recognizable musical identities.
Journey's wordless, emotionally resonant cooperative experience won BAFTA Game of the Year, making thatgamecompany only the third studio to win the award with their third consecutive nominated game.
Austin Wintory's score for Journey became the first video game soundtrack nominated for a Grammy Award for Best Score Soundtrack for Visual Media.
Skyrim swept awards in 2011 and became a cultural phenomenon, its ubiquity inspiring years of memes and Bethesda releasing it on virtually every platform including Amazon Alexa.
Dark Souls introduced the Soulslike genre to mainstream awareness and sparked debates about difficulty, accessibility, and player agency that continue to shape game design discussions.
Red Dead Redemption brought the American Western setting to open-world gaming with a cinematic quality that earned comparisons to the films of Sergio Leone and Sam Peckinpah.

Uncharted 2 won over 100 Game of the Year awards and set a new standard for action-adventure cinematic presentation, particularly its train sequence opener which remains a benchmark for in-game spectacle.
Uncharted 2 elevated game writing and acting to a level that drew direct comparisons to Hollywood blockbusters, establishing characters players genuinely cared about.
GTA IV launched to record-breaking sales of $310 million in its first week and received critical acclaim for its serious narrative treatment of the American immigrant experience.
LittleBigPlanet's user-generated content model and distinctive crafted-material art style won widespread recognition and pioneered the creation-tool genre of platformers.
BioShock's underwater city of Rapture was celebrated as one of gaming's most realized settings, delivering both sharp political commentary on Ayn Rand's objectivism and outstanding FPS gameplay.
Portal introduced an entirely new spatial puzzle mechanic and won the GDC Innovation Award for a puzzle game that could be completed in under two hours yet felt wholly original.

n: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess
fa: Twilight Princess launched alongside the Wii and was praised for its darker tone and expanded world, though it competed with Gears of War in one of the year's most hotly contested GOTY races.
Dead Rising's sandbox zombie slaughter in a shopping mall captured the cultural zeitgeist and demonstrated what open-world survival gameplay could accomplish on the Xbox 360.

Resident Evil 4 redefined the third-person shooter genre with its over-the-shoulder camera and context-sensitive controls, winning numerous GOTY awards and remaining on best-of-all-time lists nearly 20 years later.
Shadow of the Colossus' 16-boss structure with no traditional enemies was recognized as a masterpiece of minimalist game design with an emotionally devastating conclusion.
Halo 2's online multiplayer via Xbox Live set the template for console online shooters and its launch sold 2.4 million copies in 24 hours, breaking records for entertainment releases at the time.
WoW launched in November 2004 and quickly became the world's most popular MMORPG, defining a genre and business model that continues to influence game design.

n: The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker
fa: Wind Waker's cel-shaded art style was initially controversial but became one of gaming's most beloved visual styles, demonstrating that expressive graphics can age better than realistic ones.
Retro Studios succeeded in converting the 2D Metroid formula to a first-person 3D perspective while preserving its isolation and exploration, winning near-universal critical praise.
Deadly Alliance revived the MK franchise with a three-fighting-style system, multiple fatalities per character, and a Krypt collectibles mode that extended replay value significantly.

n: Metal Gear Solid 2: Sons of Liberty
fa: MGS2's then-stunning graphics and meta-narrative about information control sparked massive controversy when players discovered they would control new character Raiden rather than Solid Snake for most of the game.
Halo: CE launched alongside the original Xbox and is credited with proving that first-person shooters could work on consoles, establishing Bungie and Microsoft's franchise simultaneously.
Diablo II's deep itemization, act-based structure, and online Battle.net play defined the action RPG genre for a decade and remains one of the most played PC games ever made.
Chrono Cross featured 45 playable characters and a complex parallel-world narrative, winning critical acclaim as one of the most ambitious RPGs on PlayStation.

n: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
fa: Ocarina of Time is frequently cited as the greatest video game ever made, introducing Z-targeting combat and 3D exploration that defined the formula for 3D adventure games for decades.
Half-Life won over 50 Game of the Year awards and is credited with establishing narrative storytelling as a core component of first-person shooters.
GoldenEye defined the console FPS genre with its mission-based structure, multiplayer split-screen, and use of a film license, outselling the N64 itself in multiple markets at launch.
Final Fantasy VII brought JRPGs to Western mainstream audiences and remains one of the most influential games ever made, famed for the death of Aerith that shocked millions of players.
Super Mario 64 defined 3D platform gaming and the N64 launch, introducing analog stick control, camera management, and 3D level design concepts that still influence platformers today.

Dave the Diver blended deep-sea exploration with restaurant management in a charming hybrid that resonated with millions, making it one of 2023's surprise hits and a discussion about what qualifies as indie.
HoYoverse's turn-based space RPG achieved over 20 million downloads in its first three days, demonstrating the continued dominance of gacha RPGs in the mobile market.
Stray's concept of playing as a cat exploring a cyberpunk city captured global attention and became one of the most wishlisted games in Steam history before its launch.
Guerrilla's sequel to Horizon Zero Dawn expanded on the first game's complex worldbuilding with a richer cast of characters and an emotionally satisfying narrative arc for Aloy.
Kena was Ember Lab's first game, created by a studio with a background in animated films, and its Pixar-quality visuals set a new visual bar for small independent developers.
Daisuke Tsuji won Best Performance for his nuanced portrayal of Jin Sakai, delivered in both English and Japanese, reflecting the game's commitment to authentic Japanese cultural representation.
Ghost of Tsushima's Kurosawa Mode in black and white and its painterly visual style drew direct comparisons to the greatest samurai films, winning art direction awards across the industry.
Disco Elysium's million-word RPG with no traditional combat won both Best Indie Game and Best Narrative at TGA 2019, announced to thunderous applause by a visibly emotional developer team.
The game's politically charged detective noir writing was hailed as the finest game writing in the medium's history by numerous critics and awards bodies.
Celeste addressed mental health themes through its precision platformer mechanics, with the protagonist's journey up a mountain mirroring a personal struggle with anxiety and depression.
Woody Jackson's atmospheric score blended Ennio Morricone-style spaghetti western compositions with acoustic Americana to create an immersive musical backdrop for the frontier setting.
Cuphead's hand-drawn 1930s cartoon animation took three developers over six years to produce and won the art direction award while introducing run-and-gun gameplay to the aesthetic.
Keiichi Okabe's multilayered NieR:Automata soundtrack, which shifts language and instrumentation based on the game's emotional state, is considered one of gaming's greatest musical achievements.
Inside built on Playdead's Limbo with a deeper and more disturbing narrative delivered entirely without words, winning critical acclaim for its environmental storytelling and haunting final sequence.
Uncharted 4 brought Nathan Drake's story to a mature, emotionally satisfying conclusion that received widespread praise for its character writing and performance capture work.
Undertale was created almost entirely by one person, Toby Fox, and its metacommentary on RPG violence and player choice created one of the most devoted fanbases in indie game history.
Shovel Knight was Kickstarter-funded and became the gold standard for modern indie games that evoke classic NES aesthetics while delivering fresh gameplay ideas and level design.
Garry Schyman's orchestral score for Bioshock Infinite blended original compositions with period-appropriate barbershop arrangements of modern songs, creating a surreal sonic environment.
Hi-Fi Rush was released as a surprise drop directly to Xbox Game Pass and became a critical darling for its music-synced gameplay and colorful cel-shaded art direction.
Astro Bot became the first PlayStation exclusive to win GOTY at The Game Awards, celebrated for its pure platforming joy and love letter to PlayStation's gaming history across 30 years.
Black Myth: Wukong became the first major AAA game from a Chinese studio to achieve global critical and commercial success, selling 10 million copies within 72 hours of launch.
Genshin Impact was nominated after generating over $1 billion in mobile revenue within six months of its launch, redefining expectations for free-to-play gacha game production values.

Telltale's episodic adventure won the Spike VGA GOTY over Dishonored and Mass Effect 3, marking the first time an episodic, dialogue-driven game with no traditional action beat a blockbuster for gaming's top prize.
Portal 2 won BAFTA GOTY for expanding its predecessor's spatial puzzle mechanics with co-op and a full single-player narrative, widely considered one of the most polished sequels ever made.

Mass Effect 2 won the Spike VGA GOTY and over 50 individual awards from publications, praised for tightening the first game's RPG mechanics and delivering one of gaming's greatest suicide mission finales.

Balatro, a poker-themed roguelike created by a single anonymous developer, won BAFTA GOTY for 2024 - one of the highest-profile solo indie wins in BAFTA history and a testament to how one person with a great idea can reach millions.
Balatro was created entirely by one developer over several years and became a cultural phenomenon with over 5 million copies sold, earning nominations across nearly every category at TGA 2024.
God of War Ragnarok won the D.I.C.E. Outstanding Achievement in Game Direction award, narrowly losing TGA GOTY to Elden Ring in one of the most debated GOTY races of the decade.
Ghost of Tsushima won The Game Awards Players Choice (fan vote) for 2020 while The Last of Us Part II won the jury GOTY, reflecting a split between critical and popular reception that year.
Remedy's surreal action game set in a brutalist Oldest House won the BAFTA GOTY, competing with Sekiro (TGA) in a year where critically acclaimed games were spread across multiple award shows.

Gears of War won the Spike VGA GOTY at launch for its cover-based shooter mechanics and cinematic gore, beating Twilight Princess in a hotly contested vote and establishing the Xbox 360 as the definitive console for action games.
